verses.text: Walk worthy of your calling, 'endeavouring to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.' Keep unity. Humility and love hold the church together.
Walk worthy of your calling, 'endeavouring to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.' Keep unity. Humility and love hold the church together.
Walk worthy of your calling, 'endeavouring to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.' Keep unity. Humility and love hold the church together.
There is 'one body, and one Spirit... one Lord, one faith, one baptism, one God.' Sevenfold oneness. The church is bound in a deep unity.
There is 'one body, and one Spirit... one Lord, one faith, one baptism, one God.' Sevenfold oneness. The church is bound in a deep unity.
There is 'one body, and one Spirit... one Lord, one faith, one baptism, one God.' Sevenfold oneness. The church is bound in a deep unity.
To each is given grace; He 'ascended up... and gave gifts unto men.' Gifts given. The ascended Christ equips His people.
To each is given grace; He 'ascended up... and gave gifts unto men.' Gifts given. The ascended Christ equips His people.
To each is given grace; He 'ascended up... and gave gifts unto men.' Gifts given. The ascended Christ equips His people.
To each is given grace; He 'ascended up... and gave gifts unto men.' Gifts given. The ascended Christ equips His people.
He gave apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, teachers, 'for the perfecting of the saints.' Ministry's aim. Leaders are given to build up the body.
He gave apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, teachers, 'for the perfecting of the saints.' Ministry's aim. Leaders are given to build up the body.
He gave apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, teachers, 'for the perfecting of the saints.' Ministry's aim. Leaders are given to build up the body.
That we be 'no more children, tossed,' but 'grow up into him... which is the head, even Christ.' Maturity. We grow up into Christ together.
That we be 'no more children, tossed,' but 'grow up into him... which is the head, even Christ.' Maturity. We grow up into Christ together.
That we be 'no more children, tossed,' but 'grow up into him... which is the head, even Christ.' Maturity. We grow up into Christ together.
Walk not as the Gentiles, in 'the vanity of their mind,' past feeling. Old way. The world's life is darkened and corrupt.
Walk not as the Gentiles, in 'the vanity of their mind,' past feeling. Old way. The world's life is darkened and corrupt.
Walk not as the Gentiles, in 'the vanity of their mind,' past feeling. Old way. The world's life is darkened and corrupt.
Ye have not so learned Christ; 'put off... the old man,' and 'put on the new man, which... is created in righteousness.' New self. Conversion is a putting off and putting on.
Ye have not so learned Christ; 'put off... the old man,' and 'put on the new man, which... is created in righteousness.' New self. Conversion is a putting off and putting on.
Ye have not so learned Christ; 'put off... the old man,' and 'put on the new man, which... is created in righteousness.' New self. Conversion is a putting off and putting on.

Ye have not so learned Christ; 'put off... the old man,' and 'put on the new man, which... is created in righteousness.' New self. Conversion is a putting off and putting on.
Put away lying, speak truth; 'let not the sun go down upon your wrath'; give no place to the devil. Honest, calm. Anger and falsehood open a door to evil.
Put away lying, speak truth; 'let not the sun go down upon your wrath'; give no place to the devil. Honest, calm. Anger and falsehood open a door to evil.
Put away lying, speak truth; 'let not the sun go down upon your wrath'; give no place to the devil. Honest, calm. Anger and falsehood open a door to evil.
Let the thief steal no more, but labour and give; let speech be 'good to the use of edifying.' Useful hands and words. Grace reforms work and talk.
Let the thief steal no more, but labour and give; let speech be 'good to the use of edifying.' Useful hands and words. Grace reforms work and talk.
'Grieve not the holy Spirit'; 'be ye kind one to another... forgiving... even as God for Christ's sake hath forgiven you.' Forgiving love. We forgive as we have been forgiven.
